analysis_result: Fisher's exact test p-value (two-sided) = 0.0406 for the 2x2 contingency [G1: 6/8 (75%) with SHH pathway alteration vs G2: 1/8 (12.5%) with SHH pathway alteration]. Odds ratio = 21.0. Note: the paper likely reports CNV-based SHH alterations (PTCH1 loss / SMO gain) derived from methylation-array CONUMEE CNV calls, which are not directly provided in the available supplementary tables (MOESM4 clinical / MOESM5 WES mutations); the Fisher p-value was therefore computed directly from the reported counts in the question (6/8 vs 1/8) rather than re-derived from raw CNV calls. The supplementary WES mutation table (MOESM5) contains only 1 silent PTCH1 mutation (in sample M2242, not in the G1/G2 WES cohort) and 0 SMO mutations, confirming that the SHH alterations are copy-number events, not coding mutations.
denominator_used: MPNST-G1: n=8 WES-profiled samples; MPNST-G2: n=8 WES-profiled samples (total n=16). Definition: all MPNST tumors with Methylation_Group annotation in MOESM4 Clinical Data, divided by methylation group.
assumptions_made: Fisher's exact p-value computed directly from the reported numerator/denominator given in the question (6/8 vs 1/8), two-sided scipy.stats.fisher_exact; CNV-based PTCH1 loss / SMO gain calls (which are CONUMEE-derived from methylation IDATs) could not be regenerated from the supplied tabular supplementary files because raw CNV segment calls are not included in MOESM4-MOESM10; assume the paper used two-sided Fisher's exact (the standard for such 2x2 contingency tables in molecular pathway enrichment comparisons); assume the G2 denominator is 8 even though one G2 sample (M9450) lacks a WES label in MOESM4 (consistent with the question's stated 12.5% = 1/8).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: MOESM4 Clinical Data (108 rows, 15 cols) containing Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Methylation_Group, WES flag; MOESM5 Supplementary Table 2 (7457 rows, 123 cols) containing per-tumor somatic mutations. MOESM10 Fig 3f provides PTCH1 promoter methylation and expression (orthogonal evidence, not CNV).

B. COHORT DEFINITION: Filter Tumor Type == 'MPNST' -> 16 samples. Split by Methylation_Group: MPNST_G1 n=8, MPNST_G2 n=8. Of these, 8/8 G1 and 7/8 G2 are annotated WES='X' in MOESM4 (M9450 in G2 lacks a WES label).

C. VARIABLE CODING: Attempted to identify SHH pathway alterations in MOESM5 (PTCH1 mutations, SMO hotspot mutations). Results: PTCH1 n=1 (silent, p.R543=, in sample M2242 which is NOT in the G1/G2 methylation-group WES cohort). SMO n=0. CNV calls (PTCH1 deletion / SMO amplification) are not provided in any supplementary spreadsheet; they are derived in the paper from methylation-array CONUMEE analysis of IDAT files, which is outside the scope of the tabular data.

D. COMPUTATION: Given the reported 6/8 vs 1/8 in the question, constructed the 2x2 contingency table [[6,2],[1,7]] and computed Fisher's exact test (two-sided) via scipy.stats.fisher_exact. Odds ratio = 21.0. Two-sided p-value = 0.040559. One-sided (greater) p-value = 0.020280.

E. RESULT: Fisher's exact p-value (two-sided) = 0.0406 (rounded to 4 decimal places) or p ~ 0.04. Odds ratio = 21.0 (G1 vs G2). The reported alteration rate difference (75% vs 12.5%) is statistically significant at alpha=0.05.

F. CAVEATS: (1) Raw CONUMEE CNV calls for PTCH1 loss / SMO gain are not available in the supplied supplementary tables, so the 6/8 vs 1/8 counts were taken from the question as stated rather than rederived from raw data. (2) The WES mutation file (MOESM5) contains no PTCH1 loss-of-function mutations and no SMO gain-of-function hotspot mutations in the G1/G2 cohort, confirming that the paper's SHH pathway alterations are copy-number events rather than SNVs. (3) Small sample size (n=16) limits statistical power and makes the Fisher's exact test the appropriate choice (chi-squared would have expected cell counts below 5). (4) If the paper instead used a one-sided Fisher's exact test (greater in G1), the p-value would be 0.0203.
